title: pilosella brachiata subsp. matrensis
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. matrensis, also known as Mountain Everlasting or Alpine Hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and relieving respiratory problems. In herbal medicine, Pilosella brachiata subsp. matrensis can be prepared in various ways, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, or other topical applications. Additionally, it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects, making it a valuable addition to holistic health practices.
